reasoning: Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict stems from Debian-specific postinst/postrm scripts that don't execute in Arch. Deepseek correctly notes these are inert in AUR context. PKGBUILD uses official sources with checksums, and .install script is benign.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misinterpreted Debian maintenance scripts as active threats in Arch context, while Deepseek understood Arch's execution model
coverage_issues: Qwen reviewed Debian control files (src/control, src/conffiles) irrelevant to Arch
Neither reviewed all extracted filesystem paths from the .deb (limited to package() contents)
false_positives: Qwen: postinst/postrm apt-key usage (Debian-specific, never executed in Arch)
Qwen: postrm's dead conditional (syntax error makes block unreachable)
learnings: Prioritize files actually executed in Arch (PKGBUILD, .install) over distro-specific maintenance scripts
Improve context awareness: distinguish between packaged files vs those executed during installation
Flag Debian-specific artifacts as low-risk in AUR context
